dimensions.dimension_name: Emotional vs Rational Ad Processing Style
dimensions.what_it_captures: Whether the individual's response to advertising is driven more by emotional storytelling or logical claims and evidence.
dimensions.concept_test_impact: Twins high in emotional resonance may favor product concepts emphasizing feelings and benefits, while rational twins prefer detailed features and data-backed claims.
dimensions.ad_test_impact: Emotional ads (e.g., narratives about confidence) engage emotional twins more, while rational ads (e.g., clinical studies) appeal to rational twins.
dimensions.what_we_can_infer: Trust in online reviews and influencers based on popularity suggests a rational lean, but no information on emotional triggers leaves uncertainty.
dimensions.example_variation: Twin A feels inspired by an ad showing boosted confidence and shares it, while Twin B scrutinizes ingredients and checks reviews before considering.

dimensions.what_it_captures: Preference for evaluating products based on visual elements like packaging and ad aesthetics versus textual information like ingredients and claims.
dimensions.concept_test_impact: Visual-sensitive twins are swayed by packaging design and ad visuals, while text-priority twins focus on product descriptions and specifications.
dimensions.ad_test_impact: Ads with strong visual appeal capture visual twins' attention, whereas text-heavy or informative ads engage text-priority twins.
dimensions.what_we_can_infer: Q41 shows luxurious packaging preference, but Q49 says packaging doesn't matter much, indicating mixed signals and uncertainty.
dimensions.example_variation: Twin A is attracted to a concept's sleek bottle design, while Twin B ignores design and reads benefit claims first.

dimensions.what_it_captures: Whether the individual is motivated by aspirational brand positioning that elevates self-image or prefers practical, relatable messaging emphasizing everyday usability.
dimensions.concept_test_impact: Aspirational seekers respond positively to premium positioning and luxury appeals, while practical twins value cost-effectiveness and functional benefits.
dimensions.ad_test_impact: Aspirational ads with models and glamorous settings resonate with one group, while relatable ads with common scenarios appeal to the other.
dimensions.what_we_can_infer: As a student with price sensitivity but a preference for luxurious packaging, there is tension, but no direct measure of aspiration level.
dimensions.example_variation: Twin A imagines using the body wash for dates and social events, while Twin B sees it solely for daily hygiene.

dimensions.what_it_captures: Likelihood of making unplanned purchases when exposed to new concepts or ads versus deliberate, research-driven decisions.
dimensions.concept_test_impact: Impulsive twins show higher immediate purchase intent without extensive comparison, while planned decision-makers require more information and time.
dimensions.ad_test_impact: Ads with urgency cues (e.g., limited offers) trigger impulsive twins, whereas detailed ads cater to planners.
dimensions.what_we_can_infer: Q25 shows urgency-based purchasing for stock-outs, but not for new products; general impulse behavior is not captured.
dimensions.example_variation: After seeing an ad, Twin A immediately buys the body wash online, while Twin B adds it to a wishlist for future research.

dimensions.what_it_captures: How the individual's current mood, occasion, or social setting influences engagement with and response to advertisements.
dimensions.concept_test_impact: Receptivity to new concepts varies based on whether viewed during leisure, under stress, or for specific occasions like sports or work.
dimensions.ad_test_impact: Ads seen during relaxed moments may be more effective, while those during busy times may be ignored.
dimensions.what_we_can_infer: Q8 mentions smell matters in social situations like sports, suggesting context affects grooming, but ad reception context is not addressed.
dimensions.example_variation: Twin A engages with an ad when scrolling Instagram during a break, while Twin B skips the same ad when studying for exams.
